title: The Bedrock Where Archives Began

features.name: root chamber
features.desc-on-touch: Deep roots have woven themselves into a chamber. Inside, ledgers written on bark—the archive's first filing system. The forest keeps these records now.

features.name: stone layers
features.desc-on-touch: Bedrock in cross-section, each stratum labeled in the archive's own hand. The stone is learning to read itself.

features.name: stone nodes
features.desc-on-touch: Casings like the solar units, but ancient, made of solid stone. They were buried here first. This is where the archive learned to replicate.

features.name: growth rings continue
features.desc-on-touch: A fossil cross-section, each ring annotated with decades. The annotations continue forward into the present, as if the stone still adds to its own history.

features.name: columnar file cabinet
features.desc-on-touch: Natural stone formations, columnar and regular, like stacked drawers. Each holds soil and germinating seeds. The bedrock is archiving what will grow.

inscription: the earth learns to keep what it holds; the archive learned this from stone

file.name: Bedrock_Initiation_Fossil_Sequence.txt
file.note: ERROR: archive has exceeded witness. The stone continues its own notation. Dates advance beyond now.

exits.label: the veins where stone still sings
exits.to: NEW: the quarry where stone remembers being worn away
